authority.required_app_worktree_base_rule: Every app task worktree must start from the later clean integration merge commit that contains this accepted dispatch record; 99e7155e74934fd726fd07ba226966e75519f554 is only the pre-dispatch candidate.

route_contract.rework: Any Fable, Opus, or PM return goes to the same Sol owner. A returned task must be dev-verified again and receive a fresh Opus session before PM re-acceptance.

parallelism.basis: G-006 and R1 proved six Heca agents after serialized worktree creation; this wave has four disjoint app task path sets and two separate dependency repositories.
parallelism.r010_disposition: R-010 is intentionally deferred to Wave B because R-008 owns the shared canonical JSON/digest harness. Starting both from the same base would create duplication or a shared-file collision; R-014 fills the sixth safe slot.

public_devloop.baseline_topology: FKST_DEVLOOP_INTEGRATION_BRANCH is unset. The task branches from the exact R-004-pinned public-devloop lineage, never commits to dev or the existing source branch, and does not perform remote merge or topology mutation. This explicit, time-bounded deviation from the default dev base is reconciled only through the packages repository's own dev-targeted PR, CI, and squash route at the later authorized R-005/upstream integration step.

tasks.title: Freeze substrate direct-host and process contract inventory
acceptance_criteria: A machine-readable source map identifies every supported direct-host, supervisor, framework-child, process-group, signal, detach, timeout, log, runtime-root, and durable-root argv/env/file contract by exact substrate commit, path, symbol, and line range.
Supported, unsupported, and unknown surfaces are distinct closed states; unknown argv/env and inferred lifetime behavior remain red and name their downstream closure task.
The inventory distinguishes source evidence from executable proof and makes no build, runtime, containment, or product-readiness claim.
Only the two exclusive prefixes change; substrate and public-devloop repositories remain byte-identical.
verification_commands: jq -e . contracts/step0/substrate/direct-host/*.json evidence/step0/R-003/*.json
source-citation digest and line-range verifier recorded in R-003 evidence
git diff --check and exclusive-path subset check

tasks.title: Freeze executable Workflow JSON, canonicalization, and digest corpus
acceptance_criteria: One shared app-owned canonical JSON/digest harness is introduced for later Council reuse; no parallel copy of that kernel is permitted.
Dart and substrate-executed Lua consume the same repo-local corpus and agree byte-for-byte on canonical JSON and SHA-256 digest results.
Valid, invalid, unknown-key, version, type, cardinality, string/collection bound, digest, and hostile fixtures are executable, not narrative.
Stage ids and kinds are unique and map exactly one-to-one, in order, to the selected 24-route family; duplicate, reordered, missing, and extra stages/kinds fail closed.
The existing R-007 semantic authority is preserved. Any semantic contradiction stops for plan mutation instead of being silently redefined.

The task-scoped Workflow test is green. The known unrelated historical suite remains 19 pass/1 obsolete-fixture failure in the primary checkout and 2 pass/18 ambient-path failures in an isolated worktree, exactly as pinned by R2-preflight-test-transcript.json, until R-059 repairs that harness.
No dependency/runtime/reference-package import or product/UI code is added.

cd contracts && dart analyze && dart test test/workflow_contract_test.dart

Assignee configuration and proxy-coupled scope are explicitly rejected; label mode is the only admitted claim mechanism.

Immutable evidence-comment, optional merge, and proxy-free restart capabilities are classified present, absent, private, or insufficient without guessing; missing required seams remain red for R-041/R-042/R-045/R-055.

tasks.title: Land the narrow public-devloop visibility change

the app R-041 row in plans/parallel-engineering-task-map.md

Only fkst-devbored-loop is added to the devloop visibility manifest and its exact positive repository guard; no export, library dependency, runtime behavior, package, or other consumer changes.
Unknown consumers still fail closed and the existing exact-family guard remains mechanical.

tasks.task_state_record: The main PM maintains the canonical task-state record in the app governance repository; the dependency implementer must not edit the app repository.

tasks.title: Add a safe current-Codex invocation capability to substrate
required_read_first: the app Codex identity/auth and invocation-policy contracts
the app R-001 and R-013 verification evidence
the app R-051 row in plans/parallel-engineering-task-map.md
acceptance_criteria: TDD red-to-green tests define a generic substrate capability, not an app/Council/workflow policy, and preserve existing substrate behavior outside the explicitly selected managed invocation.

The exact installed Codex CLI grammar places global flags before exec, omits context, ignores user config/rules/project docs, uses a dedicated keyring-backed Codex home plus distinct empty process HOME, and always emits an explicit role sandbox with no bypass.

Strict/untrusted/zero-project-doc posture and disabled personal skills, hooks, web, MCP, apps, plugins, goals, and multi-agent surfaces are represented by installed-CLI-compatible resolved configuration; incompatible managed requirements fail closed.

The resolved managed invocation satisfies every field of the frozen app fkst.codex-invocation-policy/v1 contract, explicitly including ephemeral session and fail-closed approval/network policy, or fails closed.

Managed-requirement, resolved-config, and tool-surface digests are deterministic and negative .agents, tool, process, ambient-env, unsupported-model/effort, and bypass probes pass hermetically without reading credentials or invoking a real product worker.

The managed capability is a first-class parameterized invocation profile, not a compatibility fork or second legacy implementation. The existing absent-sandbox bypass default remains byte-compatible outside that explicit profile because current package behavior depends on it.
Failure is typed, bounded, grep-able, and does not introduce host business concepts, provider abstraction, persistent agent state, or dependency on the app repository.

The pinned-base all-target clippy gate is truthfully baseline-red. Its comparator uses Cargo --keep-going plus the digest-pinned jq recipe so two base and two final canonical arrays are each internally byte-identical. The final head introduces no new normalized diagnostic fingerprint or multiplicity anywhere, introduces no lint allowance/configuration, and preserves an exactly equal non-owned-path fingerprint multiset under the same locked toolchain and environment digest.

Only the declared substrate paths change on a separate branch/commit from dev at 1e03c4f91d7cc9e42dff1dc180623912ea889f65. Any necessary path outside this set requires PM plan mutation before editing.
verification_commands: cargo fmt --check
cargo test -p fkst-framework --test sdk_codex
cargo test -p fkst-framework
observe cargo clippy -p fkst-framework --all-targets -- -D warnings as BASELINE_RED at exact pinned base 1e03c4f91d7cc9e42dff1dc180623912ea889f65; it is not an absolute-green task result

run cargo clippy --keep-going -p fkst-framework --all-targets --message-format=json --locked -- -D warnings twice on both the exact pinned base and final feature head under identical rustc/cargo/clippy, lockfile, argv, jq-recipe, and allowlisted-environment digests; canonicalize with R2-r051-clippy-fingerprints.jq; require internal byte equality for each pair, zero new fingerprints or multiplicity anywhere, exact equality for the non-owned-path partition, and no added allow/expect/lint configuration
scripts/verify.sh
git diff --check and exclusive-path subset check
tasks.task_state_record: The main PM maintains the canonical task-state record in the app governance repository; the dependency implementer must not edit the app repository.

plan_mutations.new_fact: The accepted absolute-green all-target clippy command exits 101 on the clean pinned substrate base before R-051 changes, so it cannot truthfully serve as an absolute-green feature acceptance gate.
plan_mutations.disposition: Retain the repository-wide red observation and replace false absolute-green semantics with a locked, structured, zero-new-diagnostic differential; every non-clippy R-051 gate remains absolute green.
plan_mutations.adr_rationale: The mutation corrects verification semantics for a pre-existing repository condition and changes no authority invariant, dependency boundary, write surface, serialized contract, runtime behavior, or product scope.
plan_mutations.fresh_opus_requirement: The fresh independent R-051 implementation reviewer must reproduce both locked structured clippy runs and the zero-new-diagnostic delta before PM acceptance.

plan_mutations.new_fact: The v1 diagnostic subset without Cargo --keep-going is scheduling-sensitive because denied-warning failures cancel unscheduled targets; Cargo --keep-going yields a repeatable full-target diagnostic multiset.
plan_mutations.disposition: Use two locked --keep-going runs on base and final, canonicalize through the digest-pinned jq recipe, require internal byte identity for each pair, and enforce the unchanged zero-new/exact-non-owned/owned-subset rules. Keep the original command only as BASELINE_RED evidence.
plan_mutations.adr_rationale: The v2 mutation repairs comparator reproducibility without changing authority, dependencies, write surfaces, serialized contracts, runtime behavior, or product scope.
plan_mutations.fresh_opus_requirement: The fresh independent R-051 reviewer must reexecute two base and two final --keep-going runs with the pinned recipe and reproduce every differential and absolute-green gate before PM acceptance.

global_constraints: Exactly two primary FKST dependencies remain fkst-substrate and public devloop. devbored, github-devloop*, consensus, and github-proxy remain reference-only.
Workflow and Council remain the app-owned mechanisms and authority. Public devloop supplies reusable mechanics; substrate supplies generic process/runtime primitives.
No product UI, application runtime, package graph, symlink, remote PR/merge, or product launch is authorized in this wave.
App agents may not edit dependency repositories. Dependency agents may not edit the app or the other dependency repository.
No task may edit docs/spec, plans, shared app barrels, existing unrelated tests, CI, or another task's path except the main PM updating governance records.

No task may create or edit a shared README, index, .gitignore, or other parent file directly under contracts/step0, contracts/specs, contracts/lua, or evidence/step0; all artifacts stay inside an exclusive task path.
Every artifact commit has exactly one FKST-Task, FKST-Agent-ID, FKST-Provider, FKST-Model, and FKST-Provider-Session trailer.
Credential material, keychain contents, personal dotfiles, and unredacted environment dumps are out of scope.

collision_gate.accepted_commit_gate: Before integration, compute every accepted commit's actual changed-path set from its repository base, require it to be a subset of its exclusive paths, and require pairwise intersections among same-repository tasks to be empty.

review_and_integration.implementation_submission: Each Sol owner commits one self-contained artifact and supplies command transcripts plus clean pre/post porcelain.
review_and_integration.fable: The standing advisor reviews this packet and every task marked task_level_fable_required; advice never approves.
review_and_integration.opus: Every task commit receives a fresh independent read-only Opus review. Rework receives another fresh Opus session.
review_and_integration.pm: The identified main PM checks the exact diff, tests, boundaries, dependency status, evidence truthfulness, and Opus freshness before ACCEPTED.
review_and_integration.integration: App commits are integrated only into the dedicated integration worktree. Dependency commits remain pinned separate-repository artifacts until their own reviewed integration route is authorized; the app records exact accepted commit ids without importing or symlinking source.

limitations: This record authorizes only R-003, R-008, R-014, R-016, R-041, and R-051.
R-010 begins only after the R-008 canonical harness is integrated into its base.
R-059 remains intentionally outside the RUN traceability family because it repairs contract-test fixture portability and provenance, not runtime behavior.
R-039 still blocks all product and UI implementation.

findings.finding: R-008 full dart test gate is impossible before R-059 because an unrelated historical test requires an ambient sibling checkout.
findings.pm_disposition: APPLIED: scope the blocking gate to the owned Workflow test and preserve the exact known full-suite transcript as an expected residual until R-059.

findings.finding: R-051 omitted explicit ephemeral-session and fail-closed approval/network policy closure.
findings.pm_disposition: APPLIED: require the resolved profile to satisfy every frozen R-013 invocation-policy field or fail closed.

findings.finding: The shared-parent-file prohibition was not explicit.
findings.pm_disposition: APPLIED: forbid shared parent README/index/.gitignore or other parent artifacts under all four shared trees.

findings.finding: R-008 Lua execution needed an exact supported substrate seam.
findings.pm_disposition: APPLIED: pin the R-001-selected binary, exact test argv, package root, and report path.

findings.finding: R-041 lineage deviation and R-051 no-dual-mode framing needed explicit dispositions.
findings.pm_disposition: APPLIED: bind R-041 to later repository-native reconciliation and define R-051 as a first-class parameterized profile that preserves the current default outside the profile.

endorsed_without_correction: six-task wave choice and safe parallel capacity
R-010 deferral behind the R-008 shared harness
exact two-primary-dependency and Workflow/Council authority boundaries
collision, fresh-Opus, same-owner rework, PM, and R-039 gates

basis: All five Fable corrections are applied exactly and its unaffected architecture checks remain valid.
The six task packets have satisfied dependencies, exact acceptance criteria, disjoint same-repository paths, and pinned repository bases.
The corrected R-041 unit-test command passes 12 tests on the pinned source, and the R-051 sdk_codex target compiles on the pinned substrate base.
No task authorizes product/UI/runtime graph coding, a third primary dependency, reference-package authority, symlinks, remote topology changes, or application launch.
Every implementation still requires task-level Fable where marked, fresh independent Opus review, same-owner rework, and a separate PM acceptance audit.
pm_decision.next_action: Commit this accepted packet, merge it into the clean integration worktree, then create worktrees serially and bind six Sol owners.
